runtimeMaxUsps.replacementOutcome: No analyst workshop is needed to translate the business question into a model-ready analytics scope.
acceptanceChecks: every KPI has owner and formula

runtimeMaxUsps.replacementOutcome: No analyst has to inventory columns, types, nulls, row counts, and source fit by hand.
No analyst has to map every source column to semantic fields manually.
No analyst has to reconcile KPI naming, metric meaning, and owner definitions manually.
No analyst has to manually design root-cause navigation for each exception KPI.
acceptanceChecks: breach KPI has at least one driver

runtimeMaxUsps.replacementOutcome: No analyst has to write recurring management commentary from dashboard evidence.
No developer has to hand-author baseline TMDL tables, measures, and relationship files.
No developer has to create the project folder, semantic model, report shell, or PBIP metadata manually.
No developer has to build the first report pages and visual bindings by hand.
acceptanceChecks: all visuals bind to known fields

runtimeMaxUsps.replacementOutcome: No DAX expert has to hand-write the standard KPI measure catalog.
No expert has to manually reconcile every generated measure against source totals.
No developer has to hand-author routine Power Query ingestion and typing logic.
No developer has to redesign connector execution and credential boundaries per project.
No developer has to manually scrub generated artifacts for credentials.
No expert has to diagnose common model, relationship, and visual-binding failures manually.
No expert has to manually inspect Desktop logs before the first failure classification.
No model expert has to manually inspect basic relationship correctness.
No developer has to click through every visual just to find broken field bindings.
No report expert has to manually review every first-pass layout against design rules.
No expert has to manually draft standard row-level security roles for each process pack.
No analyst has to manually shape case/activity/timestamp event logs.
No analyst has to manually convert insights into owner-specific action lists.
No expert has to create a tenant readiness checklist from scratch.

No expert has to manually compare report inventories to find redundant dashboards and rationalization targets.

No analyst has to manually prepare recurring executive packs from dashboard data.

No analyst has to manually translate report requirements into source-system contracts.

No analyst has to manually draft the first data mart structure from a business question.
No analyst has to manually assemble benchmark comparisons for every process pack.
